iti85:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti85:2.1: “Remain focused, monks, on foulness in the body.
iti85:2.2: Have mindfulness of in-&-out breathing well-established to the fore within you.
iti85:2.3: Remain focused on the inconstancy of all fabrications.
iti85:2.4:
For one who remains focused on the foulness of the body,
the obsession with passion for the property of beauty is abandoned.
iti85:2.5:
For one who has mindfulness of in-&-out breathing well-established to the fore within oneself,
annoying external thoughts & inclinations don’t exist.
iti85:2.6: For one who remains focused on the inconstancy of all fabrications, ignorance is abandoned, clear knowing arises.”
iti85:3.1: Focusing on foulness in the body,
iti85:4.1: he is a monk who’s seen rightly.
iti85:4.2: From that he is there released.
iti85:4.3: A master of direct knowing, at peace,
iti85:4.4: he is a sage gone beyond bonds.
